factors.summary: The supply chain is vulnerable to disruptions and risks.

evidence.text: Our facilities, supply chains, distribution systems, and products may be impacted by natural or man-made disruptions, including armed conflict, damaging weather or other acts of nature, pandemics or other public health crises.
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

factors.summary: Productivity initiatives are ongoing but may face challenges.

evidence.text: Our success is partly dependent upon properly executing and realizing cost savings or other benefits from our ongoing production and procurement initiatives. These initiatives are often complex, and a failure to implement them properly may, in addition to not meeting projected cost savings or benefits, adversely affect our business and operations.
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

factors.summary: Organic growth is constrained by market conditions and competition.

evidence.text: Our growth could suffer if the markets into which we sell our products and services decline, do not grow as anticipated or experience cyclicality. Our growth depends in part on the growth of the markets which we serve. Visibility into the future performance of certain of our markets is limited (particularly for markets into which we sell through distribution).
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

factors.summary: Volume trends are uncertain and depend on market cyclicality and customer demand.

evidence.text: Our quarterly sales and profits depend substantially on the volume and timing of orders received during the fiscal quarter, which are difficult to forecast. Any decline or lower than expected growth in our served markets could diminish demand for our products and services, which would adversely affect our financial statements.
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

factors.summary: Pricing power is limited due to market conditions and customer sensitivity.

evidence.text: Demand for our products and services is also sensitive to changes in customer order patterns, which may be affected by announced price changes, changes in incentive programs, new product introductions and customer inventory levels. Any of these factors could adversely affect our growth and results of operations in any given period.
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

factors.summary: Order backlog is subject to market cyclicality and timing of orders.

evidence.text: Our quarterly sales and profits depend substantially on the volume and timing of orders received during the fiscal quarter, which are difficult to forecast. Any decline or lower than expected growth in our served markets could diminish demand for our products and services, which would adversely affect our financial statements.
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

factors.summary: Demand trends are uncertain and may be cyclical, impacting growth.

evidence.text: Our growth could suffer if the markets into which we sell our products and services decline, do not grow as anticipated or experience cyclicality. Our growth depends in part on the growth of the markets which we serve. Visibility into the future performance of certain of our markets is limited (particularly for markets into which we sell through distribution).
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

factors.summary: The company is sensitive to factors such as commodity prices, interest rates, volumes, FX, and government budgets.

evidence.text: The process of integrating acquired businesses into our existing operations may result in unforeseen operating difficulties and may require additional financial resources and attention from management that would otherwise be available for the ongoing development or expansion of our existing operations.
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

factors.summary: Cost reduction initiatives are ongoing but may not meet expectations.

evidence.text: We may not properly execute, or realize anticipated cost savings or benefits from, our Operational Excellence initiatives. Our success is partly dependent upon properly executing and realizing cost savings or other benefits from our ongoing production and procurement initiatives. These initiatives are often complex, and a failure to implement them properly may, in addition to not meeting projected cost savings or benefits, adversely affect our business and operations.
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

factors.summary: Margins are under pressure due to various operational and external factors.

evidence.text: The defense of these lawsuits may divert our management’s attention, we may incur significant expenses in defending these lawsuits, and we may be required to pay damage awards or settlements or become subject to equitable remedies that could adversely affect our operations and financial statements.
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

factors.summary: Forward guidance may be cautious due to economic and market uncertainties.

evidence.text: Our businesses, operations and facilities are subject to a number of federal, state, local and foreign environmental and occupational health and safety laws and regulations concerning, among other things, air emissions, discharges to waters and the use, manufacturing, generation, handling, storage, transportation and disposal of hazardous substances and wastes.
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

factors.summary: Management's confidence is cautious due to various risks and challenges.

evidence.text: Our businesses, operations and facilities are subject to a number of federal, state, local and foreign environmental and occupational health and safety laws and regulations concerning, among other things, air emissions, discharges to waters and the use, manufacturing, generation, handling, storage, transportation and disposal of hazardous substances and wastes.
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include economic downturns, supply chain disruptions, and geopolitical tensions.

evidence.text: Foreign and domestic economic, political, legal, compliance and business factors could negatively affect our international sales and operations. International sales for 2018 and 2017 represented 50.5% and 51.5% of our consolidated net sales, respectively.
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

factors.summary: Long-term strategy includes growth through strategic acquisitions, though success depends on integration and market conditions.

evidence.text: Our growth strategy includes strategic acquisitions. We may not be able to consummate future acquisitions or successfully integrate recent and future acquisitions.
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks, including trade tensions and sanctions, pose significant challenges and could adversely affect the company's business. The evidence highlights concerns over trade protection measures such as increased tariffs and trade barriers, particularly in the United States.

evidence.text: Trade protection measures, such as the imposition of or increase in tariffs and other trade barriers, including in the United States;
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

factors.summary: The company is exposed to foreign exchange risks which could negatively impact its financial statements due to fluctuations in currency exchange rates.

evidence.text: Fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates... may adversely affect our financial statements.
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

evidence.text: Fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates, including changes in the relative value of currencies in the countries where we operate, subject us to exchange rate exposure and may adversely affect our financial statements.
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

factors.summary: Input costs are facing challenges such as price fluctuations, supply chain disruptions, and potential shortages in raw materials and components. These issues could arise from suppliers reallocating resources, production interruptions, exchange rate changes, and overall market conditions.

evidence.text: The availability and prices for raw materials, parts and components may be subject to curtailment or change due to, among other things, supplier’s allocation to other purchasers, interruptions in production by suppliers, changes in exchange rates and prevailing price levels.
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

evidence.text: The company is experiencing price increases and potential shortages in raw materials and components.
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

factors.summary: The company operates in cyclical industries that are susceptible to economic downturns, which could negatively impact its business, financial condition, and results of operations. A downturn in the U.S. or global economy, particularly in key markets such as aerospace and defense, oil and gas, process instrumentation, or power, poses significant risks.

evidence.text: A number of the industries in which we operate are cyclical in nature and therefore are affected by factors beyond our control. A downturn in the U.S. or global economy, and, in particular, in the aerospace and defense, oil and gas, process instrumentation or power markets could have an adverse effect on our business, financial condition and results of operations.
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

evidence.text: A downturn in the U.S. or global economy, and, in particular, in the aerospace and defense, oil and gas, process instrumentation or power markets could have an adverse effect on our business, financial condition and results of operations.
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

factors.summary: Quality and safety incidents pose significant risks to the company, potentially harming its financial health and reputation. The company is involved in various litigation and regulatory proceedings that could negatively impact its financial statements.

evidence.text: We are subject to a variety of litigation and other legal and regulatory proceedings in the course of our business that could adversely affect our financial statements.
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

evidence.text: We are subject to a variety of litigation and other legal and regulatory proceedings incidental to our business... including claims for damages arising out of the use of products or services and claims relating to intellectual property matters, employment matters, tax matters, commercial disputes, competition and sales and trading practices, environmental matters, personal injury, insurance coverage and acquisition-related matters, as well as regulatory investigations or enforcement.
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

evidence.text: These lawsuits may include claims for compensatory damages, punitive and consequential damages and/or injunctive relief.
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

factors.summary: The company faces potential margin pressures due to labor costs and wage inflation, as well as risks related to labor shortages which could hinder their ability to integrate acquisitions, manage international operations, and grow profitably.

evidence.text: If we cannot hire, train and retain a sufficient number of skilled officers and other employees, we may not be able to effectively integrate acquired businesses and realize anticipated results from those businesses, manage our expanding international operations and otherwise profitably grow our business.
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.

factors.summary: Interest rate changes could negatively impact the company's financial flexibility by imposing restrictive covenants on its debt facilities, limiting its ability to incur indebtedness, effectuate future acquisitions, pay dividends, make capital expenditures, and maintain financial flexibility.

evidence.text: Our revolving credit facility and other debt agreements (each a Debt Facility and collectively, Debt Facilities) contain restrictive covenants, including restrictions on our ability to incur indebtedness. These restrictions could limit our ability to effectuate future acquisitions, limit our ability to pay dividends, limit our ability to make capital expenditures or restrict our financial flexibility.
evidence.subsection:
A downturn in the economy generally or in the markets we serve could adversely
affect our business.
